text: There is ongoing debate and skepticism about the sustainability and hype around Hyperliquid and $HYPE token, with some traders expressing frustration over missed listings and potential market manipulation by large holders like Paradigm. text: Hyperliquid is recognized as a leading platform in decentralized finance, with some narratives suggesting DeFi is evolving onto Hyperliquid. It is noted for its composability, liquidity, and integration of various financial primitives, positioning it as a key infrastructure player in crypto trading and finance.

text: The $HYPE token shows strong community and market interest, with staking surging from 22 million to 410 million tokens within a week of launch and maintaining above 400 million since. text: Notable whale activity on Hyperliquid includes a $5.99 million USDC deposit opening a 3x leveraged long position on BRENTOIL, and large on-chain positions involving $SOL shorts and oil longs, indicating sophisticated trading strategies and significant capital flows on the platform.

text: Hyperliquid maintains strong market metrics with over $4.29 trillion total volume, $1.143 billion in fees collected, and nearly 1.9 million users. text: The Hyperliquid ecosystem is evolving with increased trader activity and capital turnover, as indicated by HIP-3 Daily Open Interest rising from ~2% to over 30% since November 2025, signaling a shift towards a more engaged, trader-driven market with higher liquidity and volatility.

text: Concerns have been raised about the security risks of Hyperliquid's bridge, which is secured by a 2/3 multisig controlled by the core team. author: elfa_2046371383513362895

text: Hyperliquid continues to demonstrate exceptional financial performance with $857 million revenue in 2025 generated by a small team of 11 employees, resulting in approximately $78 million revenue per employee, surpassing major companies like OnlyFans and Tether. author: elfa_2046367498719879590

text: Highlight Clip Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum Must Withstand the Day All Core Developers Leave Speaking at the 4th Hong Kong Web3 Festival on April 20, 2026, Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum's goal is not to be the fastest chain or compete with high-frequency trading platforms, but to serve as a secure, decentralized, and reliable network. To support long-term digital assets, he noted that Ethereum is advancing formal verification through AI and pursuing quantum resistance. Ultimately, Ethereum strives to pass the "walkaway test" similar to Bitcoin, ensuring the network can function stably as a long-term foundation even without the ongoing involvement of core developers. — link

text: Lido reports $21.6M rsETH exposure, may deploy $3M loss buffer Lido stated that its EarnETH product has approximately 9% exposure to rsETH (~$21.6 million) via leveraged positions on Aave following the April 18 Kelp DAO incident. Loss outcomes depend on actions by Kelp, LayerZero, and Aave. If necessary, Lido may deploy a ~$3 million first-loss protection mechanism funded by its DAO treasury. stETH and wstETH remain unaffected, and the core staking protocol is not involved. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Kelp attributes rsETH incident to LayerZero infrastructure breach Kelp stated that the April 18 rsETH incident stemmed from a compromise of two LayerZero RPC nodes and a DDoS attack on a third, enabling the attacker to drain assets from the bridge adapter via a forged cross-chain message. Kelp emphasized that its own systems were not involved in the affected infrastructure, and said it is working with Aave, LayerZero, and other stakeholders to assess the impact, develop mitigation measures, and determine next steps for protocol recovery. — link

text: LlamaRisk, an Aave DAO Risk Service Provider, released its rsETH Incident Report, quantifying the potential bad debt impact on Aave under two scenarios: 1. Uniform loss socialization across all rsETH ~15.12% haircut, resulting in approximately $123.7 million in bad debt, with the largest absolute loss on Ethereum Core and the highest proportional shortfall on Mantle. 2. Losses isolated to L2 rsETH ~73.54% haircut, resulting in approximately $230.1 million in bad debt, concentrated on L2s. Mantle would face a 71.45% WETH shortfall and Arbitrum a 26.67% shortfall, while Ethereum Core would remain unaffected. The report also notes that WETH reserves across chains are at 100% utilization with under $20 of idle liquidity, limiting liquidation capacity and potentially amplifying risk. It recommends pausing the WETH Umbrella module, with bad debt to be addressed jointly by the Aave DAO treasury and ecosystem participants. — link

text: Sonic Research states that most PoS blockchains rely on elliptic-curve signatures and aggregation schemes that are vulnerable to quantum attacks and difficult to replace without significant protocol changes and performance trade-offs, while SonicCS, which relies only on per-event signatures and hash functions, can transition to post-quantum security by replacing its signature scheme without altering its core consensus design. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 20 April 2026 Retreat, Defeat, Repeat Just as markets were beginning to price in a tentative de-escalation in Iran, the narrative reversed over the weekend. Both sides have accused each other of breaching ceasefire terms, putting risk sentiment back on edge. US crude rebounded 8% after Friday's pullback, while crypto retraced, with BTC slipping to 74k and ETH to 2,300. Markets are once again trading headlines rather than fundamentals. The US is reportedly preparing a second round of talks in Pakistan, though Iran's participation remains uncertain. The Strait of Hormuz stays closed, keeping energy markets particularly sensitive to further disruption. Despite renewed tensions, volatility remains subdued near year-to-date lows. This suggests markets are pricing a prolonged but contained conflict, rather than a sharp escalation. Risk reversals show little conviction, pointing to continued indecision and range-bound conditions as the base case. A modest bid in vols is emerging, driven by geopolitical uncertainty and the upcoming testimony from Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh. His remarks could act as a near-term catalyst, particularly if they reinforce expectations of policy easing. Read more:

text: LayerZero reported that on April 18, 2026, KelpDAO suffered an exploit resulting in losses of approximately $290M, preliminarily attributed to DPRK's Lazarus Group (TraderTraitor). The attack poisoned downstream RPCs used by the DVN and, combined with DDoS, forced failover, causing the DVN to validate transactions that never occurred, without exploiting protocol or key vulnerabilities. The incident stemmed from KelpDAO's 1/1 DVN setup, creating a single point of failure; impact was limited to rsETH with no broader contagion. Affected RPCs have been replaced and the LayerZero Labs DVN is now operational. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: KelpDAO Incident Statement from LayerZero The attack targeted LayerZero Labs DVN by poisoning downstream RPC infrastructure, likely by DPRK's Lazarus Group TraderTraitor Attacker compromised select RPC nodes, used spoofing and DDoS to forge a message that the sole DVN verified LayerZero Labs DVN is operational. All applications with a multi-DVN setup should feel confident resuming operations

text: DeFi Funds Outflow Spreads to Solana Following the KelpDAO rsETH hack, the chain reaction has further spread from EVM networks to Solana. Several USDC markets on Solana's leading lending protocol Kamino have seen sharp surges in deposit APY and utilization rates. The Prime Market's USDC Reserve, with a scale of $178 million, has reached 100% utilization with zero available liquidity. Additionally, utilization rates in multiple vaults have spiked above 95%, including the Staekhouse USDC Vault and RockawayX RWA USDC. — link

text: DeFiLlama Co-founder 0xngmi on Kelp DAO rsETH Hack – 3 Potential Paths: Socialize losses across all users 18.5% haircut → ~$216M bad debt on Aave. Umbrella covers $55M, Aave treasury covers $85M, leaving $76M gap (can be filled by borrowing or selling ~$51M AAVE from treasury). Rug rsETH holders on L2s Creates ~$341M bad debt on Aave (mainly Arbitrum, Mantle, Base). No Umbrella coverage — Aave would have to absorb everything. Compensate using pre-hack snapshot Very difficult due to heavy fund flows and pooled liquidity. Even after Umbrella, remaining loss would still be ~$91M. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: OneKey Founder Yishi on Handling the KelpDAO Hack 1, Best case: negotiate with the hacker and offer a 10-15% bounty. 2, If talks fail, let the LayerZero ecosystem fund cover most of the loss. 3, KelpDAO is the weakest; compensate with tokens + future revenue, or sell the whole project to L0 or BMNR. 4, Aave's Umbrella and stkAAVE serve as the final backstop, but WETH depositors must not take any haircut — otherwise it would trigger repricing across Morpho, Spark, Fluid, Euler, blacklist the LRT sector, and set DeFi back by years. 5, He believes Aave can survive this. — link
